name: AXS Compression Fakeout Reexpansion v1
edge_mechanism: compression fakeout that reclaims direction and re-expands with confirmation
thesis: AXS 1h is the structurally different lane for testing whether a fake breakout from compression can reverse and re-expand more profitably than a plain first-break entry. The edge should come from rejecting the wrong-side break and joining the corrected move.
invalidation_condition: Abort if fakeouts are too sparse, if confirmation arrives too late after the reclaim, or if high-beta noise destroys the post-fakeout edge.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Compression resolves the wrong way first, loses acceptance, and then reclaims in the opposite direction with a tradable re-expansion leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In choppy 1h environments, false initial breaks are common. AXS should exaggerate that behavior enough to produce testable fakeout-to-reexpansion sequences.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 1h is useful because its higher beta should produce clearer fakeout amplitude than ETH or BTC, while still remaining inside the supported basket.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the lane is only noise, fakeouts will not transition into orderly re-expansion and the setup becomes churn.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the fakeout idea is right but confirmation is late, simplify exits and reduce hold time before altering the reclaim condition.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Patchy but meaningful fakeout reversals where first-target harvesting matters more than long runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: AXS 1h and one cleaner cross-lane produce a tradable number of fakeout-to-reexpansion events with positive post-cost outcomes.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or fakeouts that never convert into directional re-expansion.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; compression, reclaim, and expansion are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed through moderate compression definitions rather than extreme rarity

validation_basket.why_included: Best stress-test lane for fakeout-to-reexpansion logic in the current basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: A tradable number of fakeout reversals convert into real re-expansion.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The lane is too noisy or fakeouts fail to follow through.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Clear wrong-side breaks that reverse and deliver one corrected directional leg.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same fakeout mechanism survives on a cleaner primary lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH prints the same pattern with lower noise and acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The mechanism only exists on AXS volatility.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner fakeout reversals than AXS.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the fakeout concept survives a slower compression regime.
validation_basket.continue_if: 4h still produces enough fakeout-reexpansion events to matter.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The slower lane is too sparse.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Much fewer but larger fakeout reversals than 1h.
